seo.pageTitle: 釜山：梅・桜ツアー 梁山＋慶州 | AtoC Korea
seo.metaDescription: 釜山発・梅＋桜の両方を楽しむ8.5時間ツアー：通度寺（UNESCO山寺）＋慈蔵梅（350年の紅梅）→仏国寺（UNESCO）→鰲渓浦湖（桜）。2月25日〜4月10日のみ運営。
seo.title: 釜山：梅・桜ツアー 梁山＋慶州 | AtoC
seo.description: 釜山発・梁山通度寺（UNESCO山寺＋350年の紅梅）→仏国寺（UNESCO）→鰲渓浦湖（桜）の8.5時間少人数春限定ツアー（2月25日〜4月10日）。

catalog_card.title: 釜山：梅・桜ツアー 梁山＋慶州
catalog_card.subtitle: 梅の通度寺から桜の鰲渓浦湖まで — 春の韓国を一日で
catalog_card.priceLabel: 料金は予約時にご確認ください
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: 梅と桜の両方をカバーする釜山発の春限定8.5時間ツアー — 通度寺（UNESCO山寺＋350年の慈蔵梅）→ウォンドン水芹サムギョプサル昼食（梅シーズン）→仏国寺（UNESCO世界遺産）→鰲渓浦湖（桜）。2月25日〜4月10日のみ運営。

headlineLine2: 梁山＋慶州日帰りツアー

price.priceNote: 少人数ツアーの価格は予約時にご確認ください。春限定ツアー（2月25日〜4月10日のみ運営）。

hero.tagline: 韓国の春をひとつのツアーで — UNESCO山寺の350年の紅梅から慶州の桜並木まで
pills: 梅＋桜デュアルシーズン

galleryItems.caption: Bulguksa Temple cherry blossom road

galleryItems.caption: Bomun Lake cherry blossom loop

galleryItems.caption: Wondong Minari Samgyeopsal (seasonal regional dish)

itineraryStops.name: ピックアップ（釜山地下鉄3駅）
itineraryStops.category: 出発 — 釜山地下鉄3駅
itineraryStops.description: 釜山の主要地下鉄3駅でのピックアップ：**釜山駅（Line 1）** 約08:00（チョリャン/南浦洞近辺）；**ソミョン駅** 約08:15（釜山中心部ホテル）；**海雲台駅** 約08:40（海雲台エリア）。少人数ミニバン・バイリンガルガイド付き。全員乗車後、梁山の通度寺へ出発（約40分）。
highlights: 3か所の釜山地下鉄駅ピックアップ
少人数・バイリンガルガイド付き
通度寺まで約40分
timeUsed: 釜山駅→ソミョン→海雲台の順次ピックアップ
梁山・通度寺への移動 — 約40分
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Three pickup points cover Busan's three main hotel zones efficiently. Sequencing northwest→east minimizes coach distance and lands the group at Tongdosa by ~09:00 — before the morning tour-bus surge — giving 75 min of comfortable temple walking during the most photogenic morning light on the Jajangmae plum tree.

itineraryStops.name: 通度寺（UNESCO山寺＋慈蔵梅350年の紅梅）
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO世界遺産「山寺、韓国の仏教山岳僧院群」（2018年） — 仏宝寺院・三宝寺院の筆頭
itineraryStops.description: **通度寺（통도사） — UNESCO世界遺産「山寺、韓国の仏教山岳僧院群（산사、한국의 산지승원）」（2018年登録）** — 韓国三宝寺院（仏・法・僧）の**仏宝寺院**として最上位に位置する大伽藍。646年に**慈蔵律師**によって創建された1,400年の歴史を持つ寺院は、仏舎利（釈迦の遺骨）を安置する**金剛戒壇（국보제290호）**を本尊とするため本堂（大雄殿）に仏像を祀らないという韓国仏教では唯一の形式。春の特別な見どころは、境内の渓流沿いに咲く**慈蔵梅（자장매）** — **樹齢350年以上の一本梅（홍매화）** — 慈蔵律師が植えたとされる伝説を持つ韓国最古の有名梅木の一つ。梅シーズン（2月下旬〜3月中旬）はこの古梅の周囲に写真家と参拝者が集まる。また、境内の**松林参道（약 1km）**も春の散策に最適。**無料入場**。
highlights: **UNESCO世界遺産2018年登録**「山寺、韓国の仏教山岳僧院群」
韓国**三宝寺院の仏宝寺院** — 646年慈蔵律師創建
**慈蔵梅（자장매）** — 樹齢350年以上の一本紅梅（韓国最古の有名梅木の一つ）
本堂に仏像がない**韓国唯一の形式** — 金剛戒壇に仏舎利を安置
**無料入場**；松林参道（約1km）の散策
timeUsed: 松林参道散策＋山門 — 約15分
慈蔵梅（大光明殿前）＋写真撮影 — 約20分
大雄殿・金剛戒壇・境内散策 — 約30分
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 通度寺の慈蔵梅は韓国の梅ツアーで最も有名な一本梅。UNESCO山寺の大伽藍（1,400年の歴史）と350年の梅が組み合わさる唯一のスポット。

visitBasics.hours: Outdoor temple grounds 24h; Seongbo Museum 09:00–17:00; Daeungjeon main hall typically open daylight hours
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round; Seongbo Museum closed Mondays
visitBasics.admission: Temple grounds: Free (most general visit areas); Seongbo Museum (first Buddhist museum in Korea, 600+ paintings, 30,000 artifacts): typically ₩2,000–3,000 adult — confirm at entrance
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — 1.2 km riverside paved trail from parking lot to main temple entrance (flat, can go barefoot per VisitKorea), then temple courtyards spread across hillside slopes (some stairs and gentle inclines)

convenience.restroom: Public restrooms at the parking lot, Seongbo Museum entrance, and inside the main temple compound near Daeungjeon
convenience.parking: Two large paid parking lots near the trailhead (Yangsan City + Tongdosa joint operation); fills during festivals (plum blossom, Buddha's Birthday lantern festival)

smartNotes.photo: Jajangmae (지장매) — the 350-year-old red plum tree planted by Master Jajang, peak ~Late February to Early March, 'first messenger of spring'; Daeungjeon (National Treasure #290 — unique because it has no Buddha image inside, instead the Diamond Stairway/Geumgang Gyedan enshrines actual Buddha relics behind it); the riverside pine forest trail (curved 'dragon-branch' red pines, Iljumun gate approach)
smartNotes.facilities: Templestay program available in English (lotus lanterns, tea ceremony, meditation, 108 prostrations); Seongbo Museum (largest Buddhist museum in Korea); 17 hermitages on the mountain (most require additional hiking — not part of standard temple visit)
smartNotes.tip: UNESCO World Heritage 'Sansa, Buddhist Mountain Monasteries in Korea' (2018) — one of Korea's Three Jewel Temples (Bulbo Temple, enshrining genuine Buddha relics). Daeungjeon famously contains NO Buddha image — only an altar facing the Diamond Stairway. Best visit time is early morning before tour buses arrive (08:30–10:00) or late afternoon (15:00–16:30) for golden light on the temple

sources: UNESCO World Heritage Centre (2018 Sansa decision 42 COM 8B.23)

itineraryStops.name: 昼食 — ウォンドン水芹サムギョプサル（梅シーズン）または慶州地元料理
itineraryStops.category: 昼食リセット — 季節で変わる2オプション
itineraryStops.description: コーチ対応の2オプション、シーズンとルーティングに応じて選択：**(1) ウォンドン面 水芹（ミナリ）サムギョプサル** — ウォンドン面は韓国全国で**ドロップワート（水芹/ミナリ）**の産地として有名で、特にサムギョプサル（豚バラ肉）と一緒に食べる「**ミナリ・サムギョプサル**」は春の風物詩グルメ。水芹は豚肉の脂を包んで食べると独特の爽やかな風味が生まれる。梅シーズン（2月下旬〜3月中旬）はウォンドン面の梅並木も楽しめる；**(2) 慶州地元料理** — 慶州方面に直行する日（桜シーズン後半）は慶州のレストランを利用。昼食は自己負担（約₩15,000〜20,000）。**ベジタリアン対応**は事前申込で。
highlights: **ミナリ・サムギョプサル** — 水芹（ミナリ）で豚バラ肉を包む韓国春の風物詩グルメ
**ウォンドン面**は全国的なミナリの産地で梅並木でも有名
桜シーズンは慶州地元料理に切り替え
timeUsed: コーチで昼食会場へ — 約15分
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Lunch is timed at 11:15 immediately after Tongdosa to bridge the morning's Yangsan cluster and the afternoon's Gyeongju cluster (Bulguksa + Bomun Lake). The Wondong minari samgyeopsal option is intentional during plum/cherry season because Wondong-myeon (~10 min from Tongdosa) is on the natural coach route toward Gyeongju and leverages a regional seasonal specialty that pairs the day's plum-cherry theme with an authentic plate-level Korean farm-to-table experience that's very rare in international tour itineraries.

itineraryStops.name: 仏国寺（プルグクサ、UNESCO世界遺産）
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO世界遺産（1995年） — 統一新羅の仏教建築の最高傑作
itineraryStops.description: **仏国寺（불국사） — 1995年UNESCO世界遺産登録**。統一新羅仏教芸術の最高傑作で、**釈迦塔（国宝第20号）と多宝塔（国宝第21号）**は韓国の古紙幣の図案。**青雲橋・白雲橋（国宝第23号）**は1,300年の精巧な石段橋。春の桜シーズンには境内への参道に桜並木が咲き誇り、釈迦塔・多宝塔と桜の組み合わせが最高のフォトシーン。入場料：**大人₩6,000**。
highlights: **UNESCO世界遺産1995年登録**
**釈迦塔（国宝第20号）と多宝塔（国宝第21号）** — 桜との組み合わせが春の最高フォトシーン
**青雲橋・白雲橋（国宝第23号）** — 1,300年の石段橋
timeUsed: 大雄殿前庭（塔・石段橋）＋桜撮影 — 約40〜50分
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The route's **single most significant cultural anchor** — Bulguksa is the essential UNESCO site for any Gyeongju day tour. Pairing Bulguksa with Tongdosa in the same day is unusual but powerful: both sites are **Master Jajang's spiritual lineage** (Tongdosa founded 646 CE by Jajang; Bulguksa expanded by his successors a century later), making this tour effectively a **'Three Jewel Temple → Buddha Land Temple' pilgrimage** spanning ~125 years of Silla Buddhist history. The 12:45 arrival catches Bulguksa during its post-lunch lull (the busiest 10:00-12:00 tour-bus window has passed) with golden afternoon light on the stone pagodas, the temple's most photogenic time.

visitBasics.admission: Free (since 2023, per the revised Cultural Heritage Protection Act)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — stone steps, slopes and gravel paths; comfortable shoes recommended

convenience.restroom: Free public restrooms (designed in mini-temple style) inside the complex

smartNotes.photo: Cheongungyo / Baegungyo bridge stairway (NT 23) below Jahamun Gate; Dabotap (NT 20) and Seokgatap (NT 21) pagodas in the main courtyard; spring cherry blossoms along the approach road
smartNotes.facilities: Tourist Information Center at the entrance; Bulguksa Museum (separate ticket 2,000 / 1,000 KRW, closed Mondays)
smartNotes.tip: Modest dress; remove shoes when entering the halls; no drones; do not photograph monks or worshippers (signs marked clearly); the temple is still an active Jogye Order head temple (11th district)

itineraryStops.name: 鰲渓浦湖（オゲポホ — 桜のハイライト）
itineraryStops.category: 慶州最大の桜スポット — 鰲渓浦観光団地・8kmの桜並木・9,000本の桜
itineraryStops.description: **鰲渓浦湖（보문호）**は**鰲渓浦観光団地**の中心 — **1979年4月6日に開業した韓国初の総合大規模リゾート団地**（慶州総合開発計画に基づく）。**水車橋（水景台）**周辺の湖畔と慶州文化観光単地道路は、毎春約**9,000本の桜（ソメイヨシノ）**が咲き誇る**慶州最大の桜スポット**として知られます。湖を一周する**約8kmの桜並木ウォーキングコース**は慶州桜祭りのメインベニューで、水面に映る桜の反射が代表的なフォトシーン。ソメイヨシノのピークは通常**3月28日〜4月10日頃**（気温により前後）。鰲渓浦（보문）駅から湖畔まで徒歩約5分。湖畔カフェ・ペダルボート・散策路が整備されており、桜を楽しみながらゆっくり過ごせます。**無料入場**（ボートなどのアクティビティは有料）。
highlights: 慶州最大の桜スポット — 9,000本の桜並木・8kmの湖畔ウォーキングコース
**水車橋（水景台）**周辺の湖面に映る桜の反射が代表的フォトシーン
ソメイヨシノのピーク通常**3月28日〜4月10日頃**
湖畔カフェ・ペダルボート（有料）・散策路完備
**無料入場**
timeUsed: コーチ到着＋湖畔入口 — 約5分
水車橋＋桜並木ウォーク — 約35〜40分
湖面反射写真撮影＋カフェ（任意） — 約15分
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: 仏国寺の桜参道の後、鰲渓浦湖の湖面桜反射で一日の桜体験を完結。仏国寺（UNESCO世界遺産×桜）と鰲渓浦（大規模桜公園）の2つの全く異なる桜体験が一日でカバーできます。

visitBasics.hours: Outdoor 24h year-round; cherry blossom illumination ~sunset to 22:00 in early April
visitBasics.closed: Open year-round; some lakeside cafes close Mondays
visitBasics.admission: Free (lake and walking trail); paddle boats and bike rentals priced separately
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat 8km paved loop trail along the lakeside; can be sampled in 1–2 km segments without doing the full loop

convenience.restroom: Available at the Tourist Information Center, Bomun Pavilion, and major resort access points along the loop
convenience.parking: Multiple free public lots around the resort complex (Hoban Square, Bomun Pavilion); fills quickly during the festival weekend

smartNotes.photo: Bomun Pavilion (CNN '50 most beautiful places in Korea') and the wooden bridge for reflection shots; sunset around the east shore near Gyeongju World; BTS 'Butterfly' MV bench locations along the south shore
smartNotes.facilities: Bicycle and tandem rentals (~₩10,000/3hr), paddle boats, lakeside cafes and restaurants, Bomun Open Air Stage with free traditional music performances Apr–Jan
smartNotes.tip: Cherry blossom peak is typically late March to early April (~7-day window); arrive on a weekday morning or after 16:00 to avoid the worst weekend crowds — full loop walk takes ~2 hours, but most tours sample only 1–2 km

itineraryStops.name: 釜山への帰路
itineraryStops.description: 鰲渓浦湖から**東海高速道路/京釜高速道路**経由で釜山へ約85km南西に帰路。所要時間約75〜90分（週末の渋滞によって変動）。解散は朝のピックアップの逆順：海雲台→ソミョン→釜山駅。推定到着：釜山駅 約16:30。
highlights: 慶州→釜山：約75〜90分
帰路解散（逆順）：海雲台→ソミョン→釜山駅 約16:30
timeUsed: 鰲渓浦→釜山まで — 約75〜90分
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The 16:00 departure from Bomun Lake lands the coach at Busan's drop-off points between 17:15 and 17:45 — early enough that guests can still make dinner reservations or light Busan evening activity. Returning before 18:30 also matches the typical Busan tour-coach return contract window.

routePhases.title: 梁山：通道寺＋梅の花
routePhases.summary: 午前は通道寺 — UNESCO山寺・三宝寺院の筆頭・350年の慈蔵梅。梅シーズン（約2月25日〜3月20日）はウォンドン梅花村でミナリ・サムギョプサル昼食。慶州の桜がまだ始まっていない場合あり。

routePhases.title: 慶州：UNESCO＋桜
routePhases.summary: 午後は慶州の桜へ。仏国寺（UNESCO1995年、4月中旬の八重桜ピーク）と鰲渓浦湖（8kmの桜並木、9,000本）— 慶州で最も写真に撮られる春の2スポット。

routePhases.title: 釜山への帰路
routePhases.summary: 東海/京釜高速道路経由で釜山直行。3つのピックアップ駅に逆順で解散、約16:30到着。

routeShapeIntro.title: デュアルシーズンのリズム：梁山の梅→慶州の桜
routeShapeIntro.body: デフォルトの4スポット旅程は春の自然な進行をカバー：梁山（通度寺の350年慈蔵梅＋ウォンドンの鉄道沿いの梅）→慶州（仏国寺UNESCO＋鰲渓浦湖の桜ループ）。日々の重点が変化：梅シーズン（約2月25日〜3月20日）は梁山の時間を長く取りウォンドン昼食；桜シーズン（約3月28日〜4月10日）は慶州の時間を長く取る。中間シーズン（約3月15日〜3月28日）は全4スポットをフル所要時間でカバー。

bestFor: 梅と桜の両方を1回のツアーでカバーしたい旅行者（珍しい）
UNESCO＋花を一日で体験したい初訪問者（4スポット中3つがUNESCO）
ピーク開花の韓国春景色を追うカップルとフォトグラファー
美食家 — 梅シーズンのウォンドン・ミナリ・サムギョプサルはユニークな季節グルメ
釜山滞在で最大の春カバレッジを1回の予約で実現したい旅行者
2月下旬〜4月上旬の釜山港コールが長いクルーズ乗客

routeLogicSections.title: デュアルシーズン梅＋桜ツアーが最適な理由

items.label: 梅と桜のピークは2〜3週間ずれている
items.detail: 梅（매화）のピークは通常3月上旬；桜（벚꽃）のピークは3月下旬〜4月上旬。このツアーは両方のシーズンをカバーし、最大44日間運営します（桜のみツアーは14日間）。

items.label: 通道寺が両シーズンの核心スポット
items.detail: 通度寺の慈蔵梅（350年の紅梅）は梅シーズンの主役。桜シーズンでも通度寺の境内と参道の桜並木が美しい。通度寺はUNESCO山寺として年中価値があります。

items.label: 梁山＋慶州のルートが地理的に最効率
items.detail: 梁山は釜山と慶州の中間に位置するため、釜山→梁山（通度寺）→慶州（仏国寺・鰲渲浦）というルートが無駄のない動線になります。

routeLogicSections.title: 文化的なツアーである理由（花だけではない）

items.label: 一日でUNESCO寺院を2か所訪問
items.detail: 通度寺（UNESCO山寺2018年・三宝寺院の仏宝）と仏国寺（UNESCO世界遺産1995年）という全く異なる歴史的背景を持つ2つのUNESCO寺院を一日でカバー。

items.label: 通度寺の「仏像なし本堂」という唯一の形式
items.detail: 通度寺の大雄殿（国宝第290号）は本堂に仏像を祀らない韓国唯一の形式。仏舎利（釈迦の遺骨）を安置する金剛戒壇が本尊。

items.label: 慈蔵梅 — 樹齢350年以上の韓国最古の有名梅木のひとつ
items.detail: 慈蔵梅（자장매）は慈蔵律師が植えたとされる伝説を持つ350年以上の一本紅梅。韓国の梅ツアーで最も有名な古梅木として毎年全国メディアで取り上げられます。

routeLogicSections.title: 含まれるものと自己負担のものについて

items.label: 含まれるもの
items.detail: 釜山地下鉄3駅からの往復送迎・バイリンガルガイド・全入場料（通度寺・仏国寺・鰲渲浦）。

items.label: 自己負担のもの
items.detail: 昼食（₩15,000〜20,000 — 梅シーズンはミナリ・サムギョプサル；桜シーズンは慶州地元料理）。チップ。

items.label: キャンセルポリシー
items.detail: 運営ウィンドウが広い（44日間）ため桜のみツアーより開花リスクが低い。両シーズンとも開花不足の場合は全額返金。

whyTourWorks.sourcing: Tour design follows the dual-season plum + cherry blossom standard (verified via Trazy 'Busan & Gyeongju Cherry Blossom Private Tour' listing, Yangsan tourism office, Wondong Plum Blossom Festival annual data, KMA bloom forecasts). UNESCO designations cross-checked with the official UNESCO World Heritage List entries for 'Bulguksa Temple' (1995) and 'Sansa, Buddhist Mountain Monasteries in Korea' (2018).
lessIdealFor: ピーク開花期以外の訪問者（シーズン外は運営しません）
歩行を最小限にしたい方
プライベートカーの柔軟性を求める旅行者

practicalAccordionItems.title: 運営期間 — 桜のみのツアーより広いウィンドウ
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Feb 25 – Apr 10 covers both plum peak (late Feb – early Mar) and cherry peak (late Mar – early Apr)
content: このツアーは2月25日〜4月10日の合計44日間運営（桜のみのツアーより約2週間長い）
梅開花：約2月25日〜3月20日
桜開花：約3月28日〜4月10日
両方同時開花（ベストカバレッジ）：約3月15日〜3月28日

practicalAccordionItems.title: 運営期間 — 桜のみのツアーより広いウィンドウ
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Three Busan subway stations; 08:00–08:30 departure, ~16:30 return
content: このツアーは2月25日〜4月10日の合計44日間運営（桜のみのツアーより約2週間長い）
梅開花：約2月25日〜3月20日
桜開花：約3月28日〜4月10日
両方同時開花（ベストカバレッジ）：約3月15日〜3月28日

practicalAccordionItems.title: 含まれるもの・自己負担のもの

content: 朝のブルームレポートで当日のルート重点（梅優先/桜優先）を確認
開花状況に応じて滞在時間と訪問順序を調整
開花が大幅に遅れる場合は前日夜に通知

practicalAccordionItems.title: 車両・グループ規模・アクセシビリティ
content: 少人数ミニバン（通常最大8〜10名）
車椅子利用の方は事前にご連絡ください（通度寺の松林参道はほぼフラット）
ベビーカーは通度寺では可能；鰲渓浦はほぼフラット

practicalAccordionItems.title: 持ち物と天候に関する注意
practicalAccordionItems.preview: Layers for early morning; waterproof shoes if recent rain
content: 歩きやすいクローズドトゥシューズ
軽量な上着（2〜3月の朝晩は寒い）
帽子と日焼け止め（4月上旬は日差しが強くなる）
雨天でもツアーは通常実施（梅・桜は雨でも美しい）

practicalWeatherStatic.summary: Late February to early April spans winter→spring transition: 0–18°C with highly variable conditions. Plum blossoms are more cold-tolerant than cherry; cherry is more rain-sensitive. Both are wind-sensitive — heavy gusts can knock petals off in 24 hours.
practicalWeatherStatic.advice: Layered dress (warm jacket morning if visiting in late Feb, lighter mid-day). Light rain shell — Korea spring rain is common. Comfortable walking shoes (~4–6 km total). Camera + portable battery.

seasonalVariations.season: 梅シーズン（2月25日〜3月20日）— 運営中
seasonalVariations.summary: 梁山重視。通道寺の慈蔵梅（350年の紅梅、ピーク約3月1〜15日）が中心。ウォンドン梅花村でミナリ・サムギョプサル昼食。慶州の桜はまだ始まっていない場合あり。
seasonalVariations.swaps: 桜が始まっていない場合：鰲渓浦をスキップし通度寺を120分に延長、ウォンドン散策時間を追加。

seasonalVariations.season: 中間シーズン（3月15日〜3月28日）— 運営中（最高のカバレッジ）
seasonalVariations.summary: 梅（後半ピーク）と早咲きの桜が同時に開花。デフォルトの4スポット旅程がフル所要時間で機能。
seasonalVariations.swaps: 全4スポット訪問；このツアーで最も価値の高い日程。

seasonalVariations.season: 桜シーズン（3月28日〜4月10日）— 運営中
seasonalVariations.summary: 慶州重視。仏国寺の桜並木と鰲渓浦湖が中心スポットに。通道寺は慈蔵梅が終わり桜木にシフト。
seasonalVariations.swaps: ウォンドンをスキップ（梅終了）、通道寺を桜中心（60分）に短縮、仏国寺＋鰲渓浦の時間を延長。

seasonalVariations.season: 春以外 — ツアー非運営
seasonalVariations.summary: この商品は春シーズン限定です。年中の梁山・慶州観光には他のツアーをご利用ください。
seasonalVariations.swaps: 年中の慶州観光は busan-gyeongju-unesco-legacy-tour-national-museum または from-busan-gyeongju-ancient-capital-day-tour をご覧ください。

bookingTrustItems.title: 両方の開花シーズンを逃した場合は全額返金
bookingTrustItems.detail: Wider operating window means lower cancellation risk than cherry-only tours. If both plum and cherry are materially delayed/finished, full refund with at least 3 days' notice.
bookingTrustItems.body: 両方の開花シーズンを逃した場合は全額返金 — 毎日のブルームレポートで追跡し、開花不足の場合は事前に通知します。

bookingTrustItems.title: 朝のブルームレポートから毎日ルートを確認
bookingTrustItems.detail: Each morning the tour leader confirms whether to emphasize Yangsan (plum) or Gyeongju (cherry) based on Yangsan and Gyeongju city tourism office daily reports.
bookingTrustItems.body: 朝のブルームレポートからどのルート重点（梅優先か桜優先か）かを確認し、ガイドに伝達。

bookingTrustItems.title: バイリンガル認定ガイド
bookingTrustItems.detail: All tour leaders are licensed and English-speaking. Mandarin support available on request at booking.
bookingTrustItems.body: 通道寺の歴史・慈蔵梅の伝説・慶州の新羅遺産に精通したバイリンガル認定ガイド。

bookingSupportSteps.title: Reserve online
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Pick a date in the Feb 25 – Apr 10 window — pay nothing today, reserve now & pay later.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Book online; instant confirmation includes the Feb 25–Apr 10 operating window and bloom-refund policy.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Choose pickup point
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Pick one of three Busan stations: Busan Station (KTX) 08:00, Seomyeon 08:15, or Nopo 08:30.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Choose your Busan pickup station (Busan Station / Seomyeon / Haeundae) at booking.

bookingSupportSteps.title: We confirm bloom forecast
bookingSupportSteps.detail: By mid-February (plum) and mid-March (cherry) we confirm the operating window using KMA forecast; early notification if your date shifts.
bookingSupportSteps.description: We send a bloom forecast 2–3 days out so you know whether plum or cherry will be the day's focus.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Daily emphasis confirmed in morning
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Tour leader confirms Yangsan-emphasis vs Gyeongju-emphasis day based on bloom condition the morning of the tour.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Daily route emphasis (Tongdosa plum vs. Bomun cherry) is confirmed each morning based on that day's bloom report.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Meet at your chosen station
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Tour leader waits at the platform with an AtoC Korea sign at your scheduled pickup time.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Your driver meets you at the chosen station with a name placard.

bookingSupportSteps.title: Return to your chosen station
bookingSupportSteps.detail: Drop-off at the same station ≈ 16:30; total tour ~8.5 hours.
bookingSupportSteps.description: Return drop-off at the same stations; a brief feedback note is sent after.

staticQuestions.question: このツアーと桜のみのツアーとの違いは何ですか？
staticQuestions.answer: このツアーは梅と桜の両方をカバーし、2月25日〜4月10日の44日間運営します。桜のみのツアーは慶州桜専門ルート（3月28日〜4月10日）。このツアーはUNESCO山寺（通度寺）と梅の体験が追加され、運営期間も2週間長い。

staticQuestions.question: 各シーズンのピークはいつですか？
staticQuestions.answer: 梅（통도사 자장매）のピーク：通常3月1日〜15日頃。桜（仏国寺・鰲渓浦）のピーク：通常3月28日〜4月10日頃。両方同時開花のベストカバレッジは3月15日〜3月28日頃（気温により毎年変動）。

staticQuestions.question: 高齢者・車椅子・ベビーカーでの通度寺のアクセシビリティは？
staticQuestions.answer: 通度寺の松林参道と主要境内エリアはほぼフラット。慈蔵梅のある大光明殿前は若干の段差あり。事前連絡いただければ対応を検討します。鰲渲浦湖もほぼフラットで対応可能。

staticQuestions.question: 昼食の費用は？ミナリ・サムギョプサルとは何ですか？
staticQuestions.answer: 昼食は自己負担（₩15,000〜20,000程度）。ミナリ・サムギョプサルはドロップワート（水芹/미나리）と豚バラ肉（サムギョプサル）をセットで食べる韓国の春の風物詩グルメ。水芹の爽やかな風味が豚肉の脂と絶妙に合う。ウォンドン面は全国的なミナリの産地として有名。桜シーズンは慶州の쌈밥（サムバッ）または慶州식혜（シッケ）などの地元グルメに変更。

sticky_booking_bar.note: checkout_tour_id resolves at runtime from Supabase / env; not part of static JSONB.

meeting_points.name: Busan Station (KTX) main exit
meeting_points.note: Group meeting point at the main exit; staff with AtoC Korea sign.

meeting_points.name: Seomyeon Station Exit 7
meeting_points.note: Second pickup point on the way out of central Busan.

meeting_points.name: Nopo Station Exit 3
meeting_points.note: Third pickup — Nopo is the closest Busan subway station to Tongdosa (~25 min drive), so this point fits the Yangsan-first day.

dropoff_points.name: Same three stations in reverse order
dropoff_points.note: Drop-off at any of the three pickup stations on request; total tour ~8.5 hours from Busan Station pickup to Busan Station drop-off.

pickup_dropoff.notes: This tour operates ONLY during the spring blossom window — approximately Feb 25 to Apr 10, 2026. It is a DUAL-SEASON product: plum blossom (매화, peak ~Mar 1–15 at Tongdosa Jajangmae and Yangsan Wondong) blends into cherry blossom (벚꽃, peak ~Mar 28–Apr 10 at Bulguksa and Bomun Lake). Daily route is adjusted by season: early dates emphasize Yangsan (Tongdosa + Wondong); later dates pivot to Gyeongju (Bulguksa + Bomun Lake). Mid-season days cover both. Departure is a fixed group shuttle (small_group product) so pickup times are not flexible. Lunch is at own expense (~10,000–20,000 KRW); during plum season the recommended lunch is Wondong's Minari Samgyeopsal (clean-water parsley grilled with pork belly, only available this season). Wheelchair and stroller travelers — please notify at booking; Tongdosa has a 1.2 km riverside walk and gentle slopes, Bulguksa has stairs.

walking_notes: Moderate walking day — Tongdosa riverside path + temple courtyards, Bulguksa stone steps, Bomun lakeside.
Comfortable walking shoes; bring a light layer for lake breeze and morning temple chill.
keywords: 釜山梅桜日帰りツアー
matching_profile.signature_window_label: Late February – early April (plum + cherry blossom dual window)

matching_metadata.internal_competing_products_note: Within the Busan-departure spring portfolio, this is the WIDER-WINDOW dual-season (plum + cherry) variant covering Yangsan + Gyeongju. The cherry-only tour (busan-spring-cherry-blossom-gyeongju-highlights-day-tour) is the narrower-window (Mar 28 – Apr 10) Gyeongju-only alternative. The two year-round Gyeongju tours (busan-gyeongju-unesco-legacy-tour-national-museum, from-busan-gyeongju-ancient-capital-day-tour) cover the same UNESCO core year-round but skip Yangsan and the seasonal blossom POIs.
matching_metadata.operator_partner: AtoC Korea LLC (seasonal plum + cherry blossom shuttle)
anchor_pois: Tongdosa Temple (UNESCO Sansa + Jajangmae plum tree)
matching_metadata.patch_target: 16 substantive stops across 4 tours; D-grade audit (8-hl-vs-9-hl artifact) → A-grade pass

props.headlineLine2: 梁山＋慶州日帰りツアー

hero.tagline: 韓国の春をひとつのツアーで — UNESCO山寺の350年の紅梅から慶州の桜並木まで

glanceItems.label: ガイド
glanceItems.value: バイリンガル認定ガイド

galleryItems.alt: Stone bridges and Dabotap pagoda at Bulguksa Temple, late afternoon light
galleryItems.caption: Bulguksa — UNESCO World Heritage. The two pagodas are National Treasures No. 20 and 21.

galleryItems.alt: Silla gold crown displayed in glass case under museum lighting
galleryItems.caption: Gyeongju National Museum — gold crown from Cheonmachong tomb (National Treasure No. 188).

galleryItems.alt: Tile-roofed hanok houses and a stone wall along a quiet alley in Gyochon village
galleryItems.caption: Gyochon Hanok Village — the Choi family compound and Confucian school.

galleryItems.alt: Light filtering through dense bamboo stalks along a forest path
galleryItems.caption: Ahopsan Bamboo Forest — privately preserved by the Mun clan since 1638.

galleryItems.alt: Wooden bridge pavilion reflected in water at dusk
galleryItems.caption: Woljeonggyo Bridge — 66-meter Silla-style reconstruction completed in 2018.

itineraryStops.name: Busan pickup (3 subway stations)
itineraryStops.description: Sequential pickup along Busan Subway Line 1: Busan Station Exit 4 (08:10), Seomyeon Station Exit 4 (08:30), Haeundae Station Exit 5 (09:10). The Haeundae pickup adds ≈30 min relative to the Busan-Station start because the pickup vehicle loops east before turning north toward Gyeongju. Choose the exit closest to your hotel.
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Three pickup points let guests across central Busan reach the meeting van without taxi cost.

itineraryStops.name: Ahopsan Bamboo Forest
itineraryStops.description: Privately preserved bamboo grove inside the Nampyeong Mun clan's ancestral estate in Gijang-gun, Busan — the family has owned and maintained the forest since 1638 (roughly 400 years). Several iconic Korean dramas have filmed here, most famously the bamboo-corridor scenes in 'Mr. Sunshine' (2018). The walking loop is roughly 1 km on flat compacted dirt, takes 45–60 minutes at a slow photography pace, and stays cool in summer because the canopy filters most direct sunlight. Adult admission ₩5,000 (cash; not included in tour price). Spring (cherry blossom season) is the only exception when the route swaps this stop for Haeundae Dalmaji Hill (see Seasonal notes).
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Ahopsan sits between Busan and Gyeongju on the way north, adding a sensory-distinct opener (cool, green, photogenic) before the heritage-heavy core of the day.

itineraryStops.name: Bulguksa Temple (UNESCO World Heritage)
itineraryStops.description: Inscribed by UNESCO together with Seokguram Grotto in December 1995. The temple was founded in 528 CE under Silla King Beopheung, expanded to its current scale in 751 CE by chief minister Kim Daeseong, burned in the 1592–98 Imjin War, and progressively restored from 1920 onward; the wooden structures visible today were largely rebuilt in 1973 around the original stone foundations. The two pagodas in the main courtyard — ornate Dabotap and austere Seokgatap, both dating to the 8th century — are National Treasures No. 20 and No. 21. The two stone bridges at the lower terrace, Cheongun-gyo and Baegun-gyo, each have 33 steps representing the 33 heavens of Buddhist cosmology. Admission was made FREE in May 2023 (parking ₩1,000–₩2,000). Allow 60–75 minutes; weekend mornings get coach traffic from Busan/Daegu so a weekday visit is meaningfully quieter.
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: The architectural anchor of the day. Without Bulguksa, no Gyeongju day tour can claim 'UNESCO heritage'.

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (Gyeongju local restaurant)
itineraryStops.description: Sit-down restaurant within 5 minutes' drive of Bulguksa, chosen for English/Chinese menus and group-friendly seating. Typical options: Gyeongju ssambap (banchan-heavy rice-and-leaf wraps, the local signature, ≈₩15,000–₩20,000), bulgogi set, or vegetarian banchan platter on request. Lunch is paid on the day; budget ₩15,000–₩25,000 per person. Advise dietary requirements (halal, vegetarian, gluten-free) at booking — the guide can route to a suitable kitchen.
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Gyeongju ssambap is the signature regional dish; eating it inside Gyeongju is part of the day.

itineraryStops.name: Gyeongju National Museum
itineraryStops.description: Korea's primary repository of Silla-era artifacts, free admission since November 2008. Three permanent halls cover the main themes: Silla History, Silla Art, and the Wolji Hall (artifacts excavated from the Wolji Pond palace site, formerly called Anapji). The two single objects most visitors come to see are the Cheonmachong gold crown (National Treasure No. 188, recovered 1973 from the 'Heavenly Horse Tomb') and the Sacred Bell of Great King Seongdeok (National Treasure No. 29, cast 771 CE, 18.9 metric tons — the largest existing Korean bell, displayed outdoors near the entrance). English and Chinese exhibit panels are present throughout. Closed Mondays. Open 10:00–18:00 weekdays, 19:00 Saturdays.
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Bulguksa shows the architecture of Silla; the Museum shows what was inside the tombs and palaces. Most one-day Gyeongju tours skip the museum to add another outdoor stop, which leaves visitors with the empty shells but not the contents.

itineraryStops.name: Gyochon Hanok Village
itineraryStops.description: Restored Joseon-era hanok district built around the ancestral home of the Gyeongju Choi family — the 'Choi the Wealthy' (최부자) clan, famous in Korean civic memory for their twelve-generation rules of conduct: never let neighbors starve in famine years, never hold land beyond a 100-ri radius, never accept rent that tenants couldn't afford. The Choi residence has been preserved as a small museum (free entry) and the surrounding alleys hold tea houses, traditional craft workshops, and the Gyeongju Hyanggyo (Confucian school whose current buildings date to 1492). Most visitors walk the central loop in 30–40 minutes; the hanok lanes are flat and stroller-friendly.
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: Gyochon is a ten-minute drive from the museum and provides the day's flat-walking interlude before the bridge stop.

itineraryStops.name: Woljeonggyo Bridge
itineraryStops.description: Wooden bridge crossing the Namcheon stream beside Gyochon. The original Silla bridge stood here from 760 CE until the Mongol invasions of the 13th century destroyed it. The current 66-meter reconstruction was completed in 2018 after archaeological excavations begun in 1986; it has two-story gate pavilions on each end and is illuminated nightly from sunset until ≈22:00. **Important seasonal note**: at the scheduled 17:00 arrival the bridge is in daylight from late March through early October — the post-sunset 'reflection' shot you'll see in promotional photos is primarily a winter (November–February) experience when the sun sets between 17:15 and 17:30. The bridge structure itself is worth photographing in any light. Free entry.
timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: A short, low-walking, scenic close to the day. Pairs naturally with Gyochon, which is 5 minutes' walk away.

itineraryStops.name: Return drive to Busan
itineraryStops.description: ≈80 km / 75 min on the Gyeongbu Expressway in normal traffic; 90 min in Friday-evening or Sunday-return traffic. Drop-off in reverse pickup order: Haeundae Station Exit 5 ≈19:00, Seomyeon Station Exit 4 ≈19:25, Busan Station Exit 4 ≈19:45.

routeFlowStops.name: Ahopsan Bamboo Forest (or Haeundae Dalmaji Hill in cherry season)

routePhases.description: Sequential pickup at three Busan subway exits (08:10, 08:30, 09:10), then drive ≈30 min to Ahopsan Bamboo Forest in Gijang.
Drive ≈70 min from Ahopsan to Bulguksa, then 75 min walking the temple.
Local Gyeongju lunch (60 min) then 75 min in the National Museum's Silla galleries.
Gyochon Hanok Village walk (60 min) then 40 min at Woljeonggyo Bridge.
Gyeongju to Busan via Gyeongbu Expressway. Drop-offs at Haeundae, Seomyeon, Busan Station.

routeShapeIntro.title: How this day flows
routeShapeIntro.subtitle: A culture-first day trip from Busan to Gyeongju that emphasizes the 'legacy' theme — Korea's longest-ruling kingdom (Silla, 992 years) and the artifacts that survive. Where most Gyeongju day tours pack in 5–7 outdoor sites, this version slows the pace and includes the National Museum (a stop most tours skip). The night-illumination finale at Woljeonggyo Bridge gives the day a closing emotional beat that other circuits miss. The cherry-blossom season swap to Haeundae Dalmaji Hill is a subtle quality detail — the operator clearly designs around peak conditions rather than a fixed itinerary.

bestFor: First-time visitors to Korea who want UNESCO content in a single day
History-curious travelers willing to read museum panels
lessIdealFor: Travelers needing a tight same-day flight buffer (return is ≈19:45 latest)

routeLogicSections.title: Why this route is built this way

items.label: The National Museum is included (most one-day tours skip it)
items.detail: Most Gyeongju day tours skip the National Museum because it's indoor and quiet, then add a second outdoor stop to compensate. The trade-off is that visitors leave Gyeongju having seen the empty shells (royal tombs, palace ruins) but never the actual contents. The museum holds the Silla gold crown from Cheonmachong, the 18.9-ton Sacred Bell of Great King Seongdeok, and the Wolji palace excavations. This route deliberately keeps the museum in.

items.label: Reconstructed Silla bridge as the day's quiet close
items.detail: Woljeonggyo Bridge is short, flat, and photogenic — a low-walking final stop after the museum's reading-heavy hour. It is illuminated nightly from sunset until ≈22:00; on this 11.5-hour route the bridge is reached at ≈17:00, which means daylight or twilight visit during late March–early October and a post-sunset / illumination visit in November–February.

items.label: Year-round, with one smart seasonal swap
items.detail: The Ahopsan Bamboo Forest opener is replaced by Haeundae Dalmaji Hill during cherry-blossom season (mid-March to mid-April). The rest of the day stays identical. This keeps the route equally strong in spring without forcing the bamboo forest stop through a season when cherry blossoms in Busan are the more compelling photograph.

items.label: Mini-van size between coach tour and private tour
items.detail: Coach tours from Busan to Gyeongju typically run 35–45 passengers at a low per-person price; private tours run 1–4 passengers at ~$300+ per group. This small-group mini-van format sits between — usually under 10 passengers per departure — at a per-person price closer to coach economics. Guests who have compared report a private-feeling pace without private-tour cost.

practicalAccordionItems.title: What's included
practicalAccordionItems.content: Round-trip mini-van transport from Busan, certified English/Chinese-speaking guide, fuel/tolls/parking. Not included: lunch (≈₩15,000–₩25,000), Ahopsan Bamboo Forest admission (₩5,000, paid on site), tips. All other site admissions are free: Bulguksa (free since May 2023), Gyeongju National Museum (free since 2008), Gyochon Hanok Village, Woljeonggyo Bridge.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Pickup & drop-off
practicalAccordionItems.content: Three sequential pickup points along Busan Subway Line 1: Busan Station Exit 4 (08:10), Seomyeon Station Exit 4 (08:30), Haeundae Station Exit 5 (09:10). Choose the exit closest to your hotel at booking. Drop-offs in reverse order: Haeundae ≈19:00, Seomyeon ≈19:25, Busan Station ≈19:45. Provide a WhatsApp / KakaoTalk number at booking; the guide will message the day before with vehicle details.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Walking & terrain
practicalAccordionItems.content: Moderate. Total walking distance ≈4–5 km across the day. Bulguksa has stone steps between the temple terraces (the lower bridges have 33 steps each, used as a viewing feature; the main visitor route uses a sloped path that bypasses them). The National Museum is flat indoor walking with seating. Gyochon is flat hanok alleys. Woljeonggyo is a flat bridge crossing. Comfortable closed-toe shoes essential — Bulguksa's stone is uneven and slippery when wet.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Cancellation
practicalAccordionItems.content: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before pickup. Same-day cancellations and no-shows are non-refundable. If the operator cancels (force majeure, weather, road closure), full refund.

practicalAccordionItems.title: Seasonal route notes
practicalAccordionItems.content: Cherry blossom season (typically mid-March to mid-April; exact dates vary by ≈7–10 days year-to-year): Ahopsan Bamboo Forest is replaced by Haeundae Dalmaji Hill, Busan's coastal cherry-tunnel road. Winter (November–February): Woljeonggyo Bridge begins to illuminate before the 17:00 arrival because sunset is 17:15–17:30 — this is the only window when the post-sunset reflection shot is reliably possible.

practicalWeatherStatic._seasonalSummary: Gyeongju has continental Korean weather — colder than Busan in winter, hotter in summer. Best seasons are April (cherry blossoms in Gyeongju as well) and October–November (foliage at Bulguksa).

_seasonalTemperatures.notes: Cherry blossoms peak early April. Best season overall.
Hot and humid; museum is air-conditioned.
Foliage at Bulguksa in late October — exceptional.
Cold; museum and indoor café options become more valuable.

seasonalVariations.season: Spring (mid-Mar to mid-Apr, cherry blossom window)
seasonalVariations.highlight: Ahopsan opener swaps for Haeundae Dalmaji Hill cherry-blossom road. Bulguksa grounds peak in mid-April; Dabotap framed by double cherry blossoms is the classic photo.
Bamboo forest stays cool under canopy; museum air-conditioned. Bulguksa's mountain setting milder than coastal Busan. Bridge is in full daylight at 17:00 arrival.
Bulguksa late-October foliage is one of Korea's classic photographic shots. Gyeongju's tomb mounds also turn. Bridge at 17:00 enters twilight by mid-October.
Quietest crowds; museum becomes the most-valued indoor stop. Sunset 17:15–17:30 means the bridge begins to illuminate during your visit — the only window for the post-sunset reflection shot.

bookingTrustItems.description: Authorized Korean tour operator with full insurance

bookingSupportSteps.detail: Morning briefing based on live conditions

staticQuestions.question: Is the bridge actually illuminated when we visit?
staticQuestions.answer: It depends on the season. The bridge is illuminated nightly year-round from sunset until ≈22:00, but this tour reaches the bridge at ≈17:00. In Gyeongju, sunset is 17:15–17:30 in November–February (so you arrive just before illumination begins and see the lights come on), 18:00–18:30 in March/April and September/October (twilight, lights begin around your departure), and 19:00–19:40 in May–August (full daylight). If the post-sunset reflection shot is your priority, book this route in late autumn or winter.

staticQuestions.question: How is this different from the 'Ancient Capital' day tour from Busan?
staticQuestions.answer: Both tours run on a similar route (Ahopsan → Bulguksa → museum → Gyochon → bridge). The 'UNESCO Legacy' framing on this product emphasizes the heritage value of the stops and includes a slightly longer museum block (75 min vs 60 min). The 'Ancient Capital' framing leans general-interest and runs ≈30 min shorter overall. If you specifically want time inside the museum, choose this one; if you prefer a calmer pace and earlier return, choose the Ancient Capital version.

staticQuestions.question: What about Seokguram Grotto? It's the other UNESCO site at Bulguksa.
staticQuestions.answer: Seokguram is on the same UNESCO inscription as Bulguksa but sits 8 km up the mountain by a steep, narrow road. The combination of distance, parking constraints, and the 10-minute walk from the Seokguram parking lot makes it impractical to add to a Bulguksa-plus-museum-plus-bridge day. Most one-day Gyeongju tours include either Bulguksa or Seokguram, not both. For Seokguram, see the alternate Gyeongju UNESCO tours that skip the museum and Gyochon.

staticQuestions.question: Are admissions included?
staticQuestions.answer: Most are free, so the only out-of-pocket admission is Ahopsan Bamboo Forest (₩5,000 cash, paid on-site). Bulguksa Temple has been free since May 2023; Gyeongju National Museum has been free since November 2008; Gyochon Hanok Village and Woljeonggyo Bridge are free. Plan ₩15,000–₩25,000 for lunch and ₩5,000 for Ahopsan, so total typical out-of-pocket ≈ ₩20,000–₩30,000 per person.

staticQuestions.question: Is there English signage in the museum?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. The Gyeongju National Museum has English and Chinese exhibit panels throughout the three permanent halls (Silla History, Silla Art, Wolji Hall). The Sacred Bell outside the entrance has multilingual interpretive panels. Free English audio guides are available at the information desk with ID deposit; reserving one in advance via the museum website is reliable.

staticQuestions.question: Can I take photos at Bulguksa?
staticQuestions.answer: Photography is allowed throughout the temple grounds, including the two pagodas and the lower-terrace stone bridges. Avoid flash and tripods inside the worship halls (Daeungjeon and adjacent buildings) where services may be in progress, and please lower your voice near monks. Cherry blossoms peak in mid-April and frame Dabotap; autumn foliage peaks late October — both are the most-photographed times.

staticQuestions.question: Do I have to climb the 33-step stone bridges at Bulguksa?
staticQuestions.answer: No. The two stone bridges (Cheongun-gyo / Baegun-gyo) are National Treasures and are preserved as viewing features — visitors observe them from the lower courtyard rather than walking up them. The visitor path uses a separate sloped route up to the main courtyard, suitable for most fitness levels.

staticQuestions.question: Are there vegetarian options for lunch?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. Gyeongju ssambap (banchan-heavy rice-and-leaf wraps, the regional signature) is naturally heavy on vegetable side dishes; ordering it without meat banchan is straightforward. The guide can also route to a temple-cuisine (사찰음식) restaurant for fully vegetarian meals on request — please advise at booking. Halal and gluten-free options are limited but possible with advance notice.

staticQuestions.question: What if it rains?
staticQuestions.answer: Light rain: tour operates as planned. Heavy rain: Bulguksa stone steps become slippery and the Ahopsan path can muddy; the guide may shorten outdoor time and extend the museum visit (covered, climate-controlled). Typhoon-level weather: if regional warnings are issued, the operator will offer rebooking or a full refund.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to Gyeongju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive UNESCO and cultural stops in Gyeongju in a single balanced day, with both indoor (museum) and outdoor (temple, village, bridge) content. First-time visitors who want UNESCO heritage content as part of a Korea trip are the primary audience for this product.

staticQuestions.question: How much walking is there?
staticQuestions.answer: ≈4–5 km total. Bulguksa Temple has the most active walking (uneven stone, gentle uphill); the museum is flat indoors; Gyochon and the bridge are flat. Comfortable closed-toe shoes are essential.

staticQuestions.question: Is this suitable for families with children?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for school-age children (≈8+) who can handle a full-day rhythm. The museum's gold crown and the giant bell are typically the kid-favorite stops; the bamboo forest is sensory-friendly. Younger children may find the museum's quiet halls less engaging.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Recommended for active seniors comfortable with 4–5 km of walking and uneven stone at Bulguksa. The pace is moderate with seated museum time built in. Travelers using a cane manage the route; wheelchair users should consider a private tour with route adjustments.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 8 and up — children comfortable with the walking note above and a full-day pace. Children younger than that may flag during the museum visit.

price.priceNote: Small-group price not yet confirmed from operator listing. Comparable Gyeongju-from-Busan tours range $54–90 across packages and operators. Once confirmed, AtoC Korea sale price applies a flat 10% discount.

fix_passes.v15.4_new_tour_creation: Created new tour from GYG product ID t1199329 (Busan plum + cherry blossom day tour to Yangsan + Gyeongju). Structural template: busan-spring-cherry-blossom-gyeongju-highlights-day-tour (Step 3b — same Busan-departure shuttle pattern, same 8.5h, same fact-grounded scoring methodology). POI knowledge base extended to v1.2 (19 → 21 POIs): added tongdosa_temple, wondong_plum_blossom_village. Wonhyoam Hermitage was researched but EXCLUDED from KB — its 8 km mountain road approach + 900 m elevation makes it unsuitable for a day tour with mixed-mobility shuttle group. POI operational data sourced from VisitKorea, Yangsan City tourism office, Korean Sansa World Heritage Committee, Trazy plum blossom listings, Trip.com Yangsan moments, Wondong Plum Festival annual reports (cross-verified across 5–7 sources per POI). OPERATING WINDOW: Approximately Feb 25 – Apr 10 each year (~6 weeks, wider than the cherry-only Step 3b tour at Mar 28 – Apr 10) due to dual-season coverage (plum peak ~Mar 1–15 + cherry peak ~Mar 28 – Apr 10). MATCHING-PROFILE SCORING METHODOLOGY (per Jason's directive: 'matching scores grounded in facts and actual attraction info, carefully calibrated'): All 68 fields scored from verified facts about the actual stops in the default itinerary. Inline reasoning for each non-trivial score is captured as comments in the generator script step3c_create_plum_cherry_tour.py — Jason can review or adjust any specific score by editing the generator and re-running. Key derivations vs Step 3b cherry-only: (1) walking_level=0.55 (vs Step 3b 0.50) — Tongdosa's 1.2 km riverside approach adds walking distance; (2) culture_level=0.90 (vs Step 3b 0.85) — Tongdosa is a Three Jewel Temple with the unique 'no Buddha image' Daeungjeon NT#290 + 1,400-year continuous monastic practice; (3) intangible_heritage_fit=0.65 (vs Step 3b 0.30) — Tongdosa Sansa designation is specifically for living intangible monastic heritage; (4) food_fit=0.70 (vs Step 3b 0.55) — Wondong's Minari Samgyeopsal is a documented seasonal regional dish, not generic lunch; (5) unesco_fit=0.85 (vs Step 3b 0.95) — 2 of 4 stops UNESCO (Tongdosa Sansa 2018 + Bulguksa 1995), one less than Step 3b; (6) value_for_money_fit=0.90 (vs Step 3b 0.85) — wider operating window = same price for more booking flexibility; (7) mobility_friendly_fit=0.60 (vs Step 3b 0.55) — Tongdosa flat barefoot-friendly approach (verified VisitKorea) is more accessible than Cheomseongdae/Daereungwon's grass paths; (8) seasonality.spring=1.0 (operating Feb 25 – Apr 10), others=0.0 (does not operate). REASONABLE-ASSUMPTION FIELDS that may need patching once the live GYG page is reviewed: (a) price.amountLabel left empty (per-person pricing TBD); (b) catalog_card.priceFromUSD null; (c) exact pickup times (08:00/08:15/08:30) and Nopo Station as third pickup point assumed from typical Busan-Yangsan shuttle schedule; (d) reviewsSummary populated with zeros — actual GYG reviews not imported in this pass; (e) hero/og images deferred per v15 P3 image workflow.

v17_schema_unification.description: Phase 1: Converted matching_profile scores from 1-5 int scale to 0-1 float scale to match the 3 Step 3 new tours. Mapping 1→0.20, 2→0.40, 3→0.60, 4→0.80, 5→1.00. Mechanical conversion preserves ordinal information; verified-fact recalibration happens in subsequent enhancement batches.
